body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 18/2026
Made under: Water Sustainability Act
Published in: No. 4 – B.C. Regs. 18 to 28, March 10, 2026
Deposit date: February 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 18/2026, deposited February 27, 2026, under theWATER SUSTAINABILITY ACT[sections 116, 124 and 127] andRIPARIAN AREAS PROTECTION ACT[sections 13 and 13.1]. Order in Council 55/2026, approved and ordered February 27, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that (a)	the Water Sustainability Regulation, B.C. Reg. 36/2016, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 1, and (b)	effective January 1, 2027, the Riparian Areas Protection Regulation, B.C. Reg. 178/2019, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 2. — R. NEILL,Minister of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship; C. BOYLE,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	The Water Sustainability Regulation, B.C. Reg. 36/2016, is amended by adding the following sections: Exemption – low-volume construction dewatering 32.1(1)	In this section and sections 32.2 and 32.3: "construction dewatering"means removing or controlling excess groundwater from a single aquifer for the purpose of facilitating construction activities, including preventing floods and maintaining safety; "diversion period"means the period that begins on the first day, and ends on the last day, that groundwater is diverted from an aquifer for the purposes of construction dewatering. (2)	A person is exempt from the requirement under section 6 (1)[use of water]of the Act to hold an authorization for the diversion of groundwater for construction dewatering if all of the following conditions are met: (a)	on any day within the diversion period, no more than 300 m3of groundwater are diverted from the aquifer, whether from a single diversion point or multiple diversion points; (b)	the diversion period does not exceed 24 months; (c)	the water supply and works of persons who are lawfully diverting or using water under the Act will not be adversely affected by the diversion; (d)	there is no use of the groundwater for a water use purpose between the time the groundwater is diverted and the time the groundwater is discharged; (e)	groundwater is diverted and discharged without causing a significant risk of harm to public safety, the environment, land or other property. Exemption – mid-volume construction dewatering 32.2(1)	A person is exempt from the requirement under section 6 (1)[use of water]of the Act to hold an authorization for the diversion of groundwater for construction dewatering if all of the following conditions are met: (a)	construction dewatering activities are designed and performed, or directly supervised, by a registrant within the meaning of theProfessional Governance Actwho has competence in the field of hydrogeology or geotechnical engineering; (b)	on any day within the diversion period, no more than 1 000 m3of groundwater are diverted from the aquifer, whether from a single diversion point or multiple diversion points;

minutes_briefs.title: BC exempts low- and mid-volume construction dewatering from water-use permits
minutes_briefs.summary: New BC regulations under the Water Sustainability Act and Riparian Areas Protection Act create exemptions for construction dewatering (removing groundwater from a construction site). Low-volume dewatering (up to 300 cubic metres per day, for up to 24 months) is fully exempt from needing a water-use permit, as long as it doesn't harm other water users, the environment, or public safety. Mid-volume dewatering (up to 1,000 cubic metres per day) is also exempt, but only if a qualified professional (like a hydrogeologist or geotechnical engineer) designs or supervises the work. These changes take effect immediately for the Water Sustainability Regulation, and on January 1, 2027, for the Riparian Areas Protection Regulation.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check if your construction dewatering project qualifies for the new exemptions and, if so, ensure you meet all conditions (e.g., volume limits, professional supervision for mid-volume).

key_dates.label: Riparian Areas Protection Regulation amendments take effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
